enonce.texte:
📝 Exercise 1: Match the supermarket locations with their correct descriptions.

Column A (Locations)

  • 1. Aisle
  • 2. Shelf
  • 3. Checkout
  • 4. Entrance
  • 5. Dairy section
  • 6. Vegetable section

Column B (Descriptions)

  • A. Where you pay for your items
  • B. The door where you enter the supermarket
  • C. A long passage between rows of shelves
  • D. Where you find milk, cheese, and yogurt
  • E. Where you find tomatoes, onions, and lettuce
  • F. A flat board where items are displayed
images: A supermarket interior with clearly marked sections: aisle, shelf, checkout, entrance, dairy section, vegetable section

questions.texte: Match each location in Column A with its correct description in Column B.

sousQuestions.texte: Aisle
sousQuestions.reponse: C. A long passage between rows of shelves

sousQuestions.texte: Shelf
sousQuestions.reponse: F. A flat board where items are displayed

sousQuestions.texte: Checkout
sousQuestions.reponse: A. Where you pay for your items

sousQuestions.texte: Entrance
sousQuestions.reponse: B. The door where you enter the supermarket

sousQuestions.texte: Dairy section
sousQuestions.reponse: D. Where you find milk, cheese, and yogurt

sousQuestions.texte: Vegetable section
sousQuestions.reponse: E. Where you find tomatoes, onions, and lettuce

enonce.texte:
📍 Exercise 2: Complete the sentences with the correct preposition of place: next to, between, opposite, near.

Information: In the supermarket, the fruit section is _______ the vegetable section. The checkout is _______ the exit. The bakery is _______ the dairy section. The entrance is _______ the car park.
images: A supermarket floor plan showing different sections with labeled areas: fruit section, vegetable section, checkout, exit, bakery, dairy section, entrance, car park

questions.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct preposition of place.

sousQuestions.texte: The fruit section is _______ the vegetable section.
sousQuestions.reponse: next to

sousQuestions.texte: The checkout is _______ the exit.
sousQuestions.reponse: near

sousQuestions.texte: The bakery is _______ the dairy section and the meat section.
sousQuestions.reponse: between

sousQuestions.texte: The entrance is _______ the car park.
sousQuestions.reponse: opposite

enonce.texte:
🗺️ Exercise 3: Look at the supermarket map and answer the questions.

Supermarket Map:

  • Entrance → left
  • Vegetable section → right of entrance
  • Fruit section → next to vegetable section
  • Dairy section → behind vegetable section
  • Meat section → next to dairy section
  • Checkout → near exit (right side)
  • Exit → right
images: A simple supermarket map showing the entrance on the left, vegetable section right of entrance, fruit section next to vegetables, dairy section behind vegetables, meat section next to dairy, checkout near exit on the right, exit on the right

questions.texte: Answer the questions based on the supermarket map.

sousQuestions.texte: Where is the entrance? (Identify)
sousQuestions.reponse: The entrance is on the left.

sousQuestions.texte: Which section is next to the vegetable section? (Identify)
sousQuestions.reponse: The fruit section is next to the vegetable section.

sousQuestions.texte: What is behind the vegetable section? (Identify)
sousQuestions.reponse: The dairy section is behind the vegetable section.

sousQuestions.texte: Describe where the checkout is located. (Describe)
sousQuestions.reponse: The checkout is near the exit on the right side.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why the map is useful for customers. (Explain)
sousQuestions.reponse: The map is useful because it helps customers find items quickly, saves time, and makes shopping easier. It shows where different sections are located.

enonce.texte:
🗣️ Exercise 4: Read the dialogue and complete it with the missing expressions.

Dialogue:

Customer: Excuse me, ___________ find the dairy section?

Shop assistant: Yes, of course. Go straight, then ___________ at the vegetable section. The dairy section is ___________ the meat section.

Customer: Thank you. And ___________ is the checkout?

Shop assistant: The checkout is ___________ the exit.
images: A customer speaking to a shop assistant in a supermarket, pointing to different sections, friendly interaction

questions.texte: Complete the dialogue with the correct expressions.
questions.reponse: 1. Where can I, 2. turn left, 3. next to, 4. where, 5. near

sousQuestions.texte: Customer: Excuse me, ___________ find the dairy section?
sousQuestions.reponse: Where can I

sousQuestions.texte: Shop assistant: Go straight, then ___________ at the vegetable section.
sousQuestions.reponse: turn left

sousQuestions.texte: The dairy section is ___________ the meat section.
sousQuestions.reponse: next to

sousQuestions.texte: Customer: And ___________ is the checkout?
sousQuestions.reponse: where

sousQuestions.texte: Shop assistant: The checkout is ___________ the exit.
sousQuestions.reponse: near

enonce.texte:
🛒 Exercise 5: Create a full dialogue between a customer and a shop assistant.

Instructions: Write a dialogue with at least 6 exchanges between a customer and a shop assistant. The customer needs to find three items in the supermarket. Use vocabulary from the lesson.

Useful vocabulary: entrance, aisle, shelf, checkout, next to, between, opposite, near, go straight, turn left, turn right, where can I find, which aisle, thank you, you're welcome.
images: A customer and shop assistant talking in a supermarket, pointing and looking at shelves, warm and helpful atmosphere

questions.texte: Write a complete dialogue between a customer and a shop assistant (at least 6 exchanges).
questions.reponse:
Example dialogue:

Customer: Excuse me, where can I find the dairy section?
Shop assistant: The dairy section is next to the meat section. Go straight and turn left.
Customer: Thank you. And which aisle is the bread?
Shop assistant: The bread is in aisle 5, opposite the fruit section.
Customer: Great. Where is the checkout?
Shop assistant: The checkout is near the exit on the right side.
Customer: Thank you very much for your help!
Shop assistant: You're welcome. Happy shopping!
